object_under_evaluation: growth-refinery lineage-chain coherence across checkpoint, reviewed harvest, seed staging, and owner evidence surfaces
claim_boundary: This report speaks only to whether one bounded growth-refinery lineage stays structurally coherent enough for later owner proof and stats to read honestly.
limitations: It does not prove that the chosen owner layer is correct.
It does not grade final object quality.
It does not let lineage structure replace owner-local evidence.

case_notes.integrity_note: The chain keeps cluster, reviewed candidate, staged seed, and downstream owner evidence in order without inventing extra continuity.
case_notes.overread_boundary: This clean chain still does not prove that the chosen owner or planted object is high quality.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Review whether the owner evidence remains as specific as the lineage makes it look.

case_notes.integrity_note: The superseded branch stays explicit and points to its replacement seed, but one owner-shape note is thinner than the rest of the trail.
case_notes.overread_boundary: Visible supersession is structural traceability, not proof that the replacement route was optimal.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Tighten the owner-shaping note before reusing this branch in downstream stats or proof.

case_notes.integrity_note: The case introduces staged seed identity before a reviewed candidate exists, so the chain collapses at the candidate gate.
case_notes.overread_boundary: A tidy seed note cannot repair a missing reviewed-candidate prerequisite.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Restore the reviewed candidate seam before any seed or owner evidence is reused.

major_review_flags: One lineage branch keeps structural continuity but still carries thin owner-shaping context.
A downstream-looking seed artifact can still overrun the reviewed-candidate gate if the stage chain is not checked explicitly.
